summary: The first compile failed because the host-side include used shufflenet_unit_custom_tiling.h, but the generator emitted shuffle_net_unit_custom_tiling.h from the CamelCase op name. Aligning the include filename with the generated snake_case header fixed the missing-tiling-header build error.

+            self.shortcut_bn_weight.cpu(),
+            self.shortcut_bn_bias.cpu(),
+            self.shortcut_bn_running_mean.cpu(),
+            self.shortcut_bn_running_var.cpu(),
             self.groups,
             self.use_shortcut_conv,
+            self.eps,
+            self.momentum,
         )
 '''
summary: Pybind compilation failed because at::conv2d with brace-init stride and padding hit an overload ambiguity. Replacing inline initializer lists with explicit IntArrayRef arrays removed the ambiguity and let the extension compile.

code_diff:
--- error_idx_4
+++ correct_idx_6
@@ -383,6 +383,8 @@
         super(ModelNew, self).__init__()
         assert out_channels % 4 == 0
         self.groups = groups
+        self.eps = 1e-5
+        self.momentum = 0.1
         mid_channels = out_channels // 4
 
         self.conv1 = nn.Conv2d(in_channels, mid_channels, kernel_size=1, stride=1, padding=0, groups=groups, bias=False)
@@ -442,5 +444,7 @@
             self.shortcut_bn_running_var.cpu(),
             self.groups,
             self.use_shortcut_conv,
+            self.eps,
+            self.momentum,
         )
 '''
summary: The bound custom op expected explicit eps and momentum arguments even though the C++ wrapper declared default values. Passing eps and momentum from ModelNew.forward fixed the pybind argument-count mismatch during correctness execution.

code_diff:
--- error_idx_5
+++ correct_idx_6
@@ -383,6 +383,8 @@
         super(ModelNew, self).__init__()
         assert out_channels % 4 == 0
         self.groups = groups
+        self.eps = 1e-5
+        self.momentum = 0.1
         mid_channels = out_channels // 4
 
         self.conv1 = nn.Conv2d(in_channels, mid_channels, kernel_size=1, stride=1, padding=0, groups=groups, bias=False)
@@ -442,5 +444,7 @@
             self.shortcut_bn_running_var.cpu(),
             self.groups,
             self.use_shortcut_conv,
+            self.eps,
+            self.momentum,
         )
 '''
summary: For this ShuffleNet unit, moving tensors to CPU before calling the pybind wrapper avoided an NPU runtime failure in the channel-shuffle path, but the wrapper still required explicit eps and momentum arguments. The stable fix was CPU execution plus explicit scalar arguments from forward.
